---
title: "CV"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    css: assets/cv.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import re
#from IPython.display import display, HTML


DATA_DIR = Path("files/cv")
YOUR_NAME = "Kirkpatrick, LR"  # <-- change to exactly what appears in your CSV author lists

def read_csv(name: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    path = DATA_DIR / name
    if not path.exists():
        return pd.DataFrame()
    return pd.read_csv(path)

def normalize_whitespace(s: str) -> str:
    # Treat missing values (NaN) and literal "nan" as empty
    if pd.isna(s):
        return ""
    s_str = str(s)
    if s_str.lower() == "nan":
        return ""
    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s_str).strip()

def bold_name(authors: str, name: str) -> str:
    """
    Bolds and underlines exact matches of the name in common author formats.
    Works best if your CSV uses one consistent representation of your name.
    """
    if not isinstance(authors, str):
        return ""

    a = normalize_whitespace(authors)
    n = normalize_whitespace(name)

    # Escape for regex and match with "word-ish" boundaries
    pat = r"(?<![\w])" + re.escape(n) + r"(?![\w])"
    # Wrap with underline and strong tags
    a = re.sub(pat, f"<u><strong>{n}</strong></u>", a)

    # Optional: also catch "Last, First" if you sometimes store that.
    # If you *never* store that, you can delete this block.
    if "," not in n:
        parts = n.split(" ")
        if len(parts) >= 2:
            first = parts[0]
            last = parts[-1]
            alt = f"{last}, {first}"
            pat_alt = r"(?<![\w])" + re.escape(alt) + r"(?![\w])"
            a = re.sub(pat_alt, f"<u><strong>{alt}</strong></u>", a)

    return a

def fmt_link(doi: str = "", url: str = "") -> str:
    doi = normalize_whitespace(doi)
    url = normalize_whitespace(url)

    links = []
    if doi and doi.lower() != "nan":
        links.append(f"[doi](https://doi.org/{doi})")
    if url and url.lower() != "nan":
        links.append(f"[link]({url})")
    return " · ".join(links)

def render_entries(df: pd.DataFrame, kind: str) -> str:
    """
    Returns Markdown that Quarto will render.
    Expects at least 'year' and 'title' plus other fields depending on kind.
    """
    # drop fully-empty rows first
    df = df.dropna(how="all")
    if df.empty:
        return "_No entries yet._"

    # Ensure year exists and sort descending when possible
    if "year" in df.columns:
        df["year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["year"], errors="coerce")
        df = df.sort_values(["year"], ascending=False)

    blocks = []

    for _, row in df.iterrows():
        year = "" if "year" not in df.columns else row.get("year", "")
        year_str = "" if pd.isna(year) else str(int(year)) if float(year).is_integer() else str(year)

        title = normalize_whitespace(row.get("title", ""))
        authors = bold_name(row.get("authors", ""), YOUR_NAME)

        if kind == "publications":
            venue = normalize_whitespace(row.get("venue", ""))
            volume = normalize_whitespace(row.get("volume", ""))
            pages = normalize_whitespace(row.get("pages", ""))
            doi = row.get("doi", "")
            url = row.get("url", "")

            venue_bits = [v for v in [venue, volume, pages] if v and v.lower() != "nan"]
            venue_str = ", ".join(venue_bits)

            links = fmt_link(doi=doi, url=url)
            links_str = f"<span class='cv-links'>{links}</span>" if links else ""

            text = (
                f"<span class='cv-title'>{title}</span><br>"
                f"{authors}<br>"
                f"<span class='cv-venue'>{venue_str}</span>"
                + (f"<br>{links_str}" if links_str else "")
            )

        elif kind == "presentations":
            event = normalize_whitespace(row.get("event", ""))
            location = normalize_whitespace(row.get("location", ""))
            date = normalize_whitespace(row.get("date", ""))
            ptype = normalize_whitespace(row.get("type", ""))
            url = row.get("url", "")

            where = ", ".join([x for x in [event, location] if x and x.lower() != "nan"])
            meta = " · ".join([x for x in [ptype, date] if x and x.lower() != "nan"])
            links = fmt_link(url=url)

            text = (
                f"<span class='cv-title'>{title}</span><br>"
                f"{authors}<br>"
                f"{where}"
                + (f"<br>{meta}" if meta else "")
                + (f"<br><span class='cv-links'>{links}</span>" if links else "")
            )

        else:
            # Generic fallback for awards/grants/etc.
            item = normalize_whitespace(row.get("item", title))
            details = normalize_whitespace(row.get("details", ""))
            url = row.get("url", "")
            links = fmt_link(url=url)

            text = (
                f"{item}"
                + (f"<br>{details}" if details else "")
                + (f"<br><span class='cv-links'>{links}</span>" if links else "")
            )

        block = f"""
<div class="cv-entry">
  <div class="cv-year">{year_str}</div>
  <div class="cv-text"><p>{text}</p></div>
</div>
""".strip()

        blocks.append(block)

    return "\n\n".join(blocks)

```

## Publications
```{python}
#| output: asis
pubs = read_csv("publications.csv")
print(render_entries(pubs, kind="publications"))
```

## Selected Presentations
```{python}
#| output: asis
pres = read_csv("presentations.csv")
print(render_entries(pres, kind="presentations"))
```

## Grants
```{python}
#| output: asis
grants = read_csv("grants.csv")
print(render_entries(grants, kind="grants"))
```

## Mentorship
```{python}
#| output: asis
ment = read_csv("mentorship.csv")
print(render_entries(ment, kind="mentorship"))
```

## Awards
```{python}
#| output: asis
awards = read_csv("awards.csv")
print(render_entries(awards, kind="awards"))
```

## Professional Activities
```{python}
#| output: asis
awards = read_csv("awards.csv")
print(render_entries(awards, kind="awards"))
```


<!-- ---
title: "CV"
description-meta: "Liam Kirkaptrick's CV"
title-block-banner: false
---

<center>

CV coming soon!

</center> -->
